ops.title: Env-sync sidecar prune: drop peer-only tombstone names, keep locally-held ones
ops.body:
Decision (env-sync hardening, growth-bounds fix): `dropTombstoneOnlyAbsent` in `internal/reconcile/env.go` prunes a file name from the merged state before sidecar persist only when ALL three hold: no present keys, no local regular file, AND the name is absent from the loaded base sidecar.

Why the third condition: without it, a *locally deleted* synced file's tombstones (created by `observeMissing`) would be pruned the same pass they were created — the host would stop serving the deletion, and a peer still holding the file would re-advertise it present, resurrecting it on the deleting host. With the base-sidecar check, a locally-held name's (blank-valued) tombstones persist and keep propagating the deletion until TombstoneTTL GC empties the FileMap and `Save`'s empty-file drop removes the name — at which point every reachable peer has long since converged. Peer-injected tombstone spam under never-local names is still rejected every pass and never reaches the sidecar, since it can never enter the base.

Determinism note: the condition reads only local state (base sidecar, local Lstat, merged presence), so every host makes its own decision independently and the mesh cannot diverge on it.

Pinned by `TestConvergeEnvWholeFileDeletionPropagates` and `TestConvergeEnvDropsNeverLocalTombstone` in `internal/reconcile/env_test.go`.
